steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The researchers' conclusion emphasizes “growing interest...across the years analyzed.” This signals we need to find an upward trend in the data showing an increase over time.

steps.title: Match the trend to the choices
steps.content: Department leaders rising from approximately 3.2 to 4 to 6.8 across the three periods demonstrates the growth in CEO connections to departments that the researchers concluded was happening. A falsely claims no fluctuation; B incorrectly identifies which period had the highest number of managers; C describes a consistent relationship but doesn't show the time-based growth required by the conclusion. That matches choice D — D is correct.
